item.title: Ba storm into Fiji FACT semi-finals with 6-0 win over Nasinu
item.description: Ba storm into Fiji FACT semi-finals with 6-0 win over Nasinu

item.title: Undav scores dramatic late winner as Germany stun Ivory Coast 2-1
item.description: Deniz Undav emerged as Germany's hero, scoring a stoppage-time winner as the four-time world champions fought back from a goal down to defeat Ivory Coast 2-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group E match. Ivory Coast took a 1-0 lead into halftime after Franck Kessie found the back of the net in the 30th minute.
item.content:encoded:
Deniz Undav emerged as Germany's hero, scoring a stoppage-time winner as the four-time world champions fought back from a goal down to defeat Ivory Coast 2-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group E match.

Ivory Coast took a 1-0 lead into halftime after Franck Kessie found the back of the net in the 30th minute.

Germany fought back in the second half, with Undav equalising in the 68th minute after being set up by Nadiem Amiri.

Just when it looked like the match was heading for a draw, Undav struck again in the fourth minute of stoppage time to seal a dramatic victory.

The win strengthens Germany's position in Group E heading into the final round of pool matches.

Germany had opened their World Cup campaign with an emphatic 7-1 victory over Curaçao, while Ivory Coast defeated Ecuador 1-0 in their first match.

Germany are making their 21st FIFA World Cup appearance and are currently ranked 10th in the FIFA World Rankings. Ivory Coast are featuring in their fourth FIFA World Cup and are ranked 41st in the world.

Germany will face Ecuador in their final Group E match next Friday at 8am, while Ivory Coast will take on Curaçao at the same time.

Meanwhile, the Netherlands hammered Sweden 5-1 earlier this morning.

Ecuador will meet Curaçao at 12pm, while Tunisia and Japan will go head-to-head at 4pm.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Basketball: Yat Sen, Gospel High, Marist and Suva Grammar record wins in pool matches
item.description: Yat Sen Secondary School U17 defeated Church College Fiji 39-11 in the Suva Secondary Schools Basketball competition. Yat Sen is the favourites to win the title but there will competition from schools such as Suva Grammar, Marist Brothers and International School Suva.
item.content:encoded:
Yat Sen Secondary School U17 defeated Church College Fiji 39-11 in the Suva Secondary Schools Basketball competition.

Yat Sen is the favourites to win the title but there will competition from schools such as Suva Grammar, Marist Brothers and International School Suva.

Meanwhile, defending U17 champion's Yat Sen Secondary went down 36-33 to Gospel High School.

Marist Brothers High School secured the Under-15 Boys' championship after overcoming Church College Fiji 19-15.

In the Under-15 Girls' division, Suva Grammar School proved too strong for Saint Joseph's Secondary School, earning a 24-19 win.

item.title: Hurricanes claim 2026 Super Rugby Pacific title with record victory over Chiefs
Netherlands hammer Sweden 5-1 to strengthen World Cup knockout hopes
item.description: The Netherlands delivered a commanding performance to defeat Sweden 5-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group F match. Brian Brobbey gave the Dutch the perfect start, scoring in the 5th minute before doubling their lead in the 17th minute to hand the Netherlands a 2-0 advantage at halftime.
item.content:encoded:
The Netherlands delivered a commanding performance to defeat Sweden 5-1 in their FIFA World Cup Group F match.

Brian Brobbey gave the Dutch the perfect start, scoring in the 5th minute before doubling their lead in the 17th minute to hand the Netherlands a 2-0 advantage at halftime.

The Dutch continued their dominance after the break with Cody Gakpo scoring twice in quick succession in the 47th and 54th minutes to make it 4-0.

Sweden pulled one back through Anthony Elanga in the 59th minute, but any hopes of a comeback were ended when Crysencio Summerville found the back of the net in the 89th minute to seal the emphatic victory.

The win puts the Netherlands in a strong position in Group F heading into the final round of pool matches.

The Netherlands are making their 12th FIFA World Cup appearance and are regarded as one of the strongest nations, however they have never won the tournament, having finished runners-up in 1974, 1978 and 2010.

Sweden are featuring in their 13th FIFA World Cup and achieved their best result in 1958 when they finished runners-up on home soil. They also reached the semi-finals in 1994.

The Netherlands will face Tunisia in their final pool match next Friday at 11am, while Sweden will take on Japan in a must-win encounter at the same time.

Meanwhile, Germany will face Ivory Coast in the next hour, Ecuador will meet Curaçao at 12pm, while Tunisia and Japan will go head-to-head at 4pm.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Ivory Coast take early 1-nil lead against Germany at half-time
item.description: Ivory Coast have taken a 1-0 lead over Germany at half time in their FIFA World Cup Group E match at Toronto Stadium. Midfielder Franck Kessie broke the deadlock in the 30th minute to give the Africans the advantage in a match that could play a major role in deciding the Group E standings.
item.content:encoded:
Ivory Coast have taken a 1-0 lead over Germany at half time in their FIFA World Cup Group E match at Toronto Stadium.

Midfielder Franck Kessie broke the deadlock in the 30th minute to give the Africans the advantage in a match that could play a major role in deciding the Group E standings.

Germany came into the match full of confidence after thrashing Curaçao 7-1 in their opening pool game, while Ivory Coast edged Ecuador 1-0 in their first outing.

Germany are making their 21st FIFA World Cup appearance and are four-time world champions, having won the title in 1954, 1974, 1990 and 2014. They are currently ranked 10th in the FIFA World Rankings.

Ivory Coast are featuring in their fourth FIFA World Cup and are seeking a place in the knockout stages for the first time. The Elephants are currently ranked 41st in the FIFA World Rankings.

Meanwhile, the Netherlands hammered Sweden 5-1 earlier this morning.

Ecuador will meet Curaçao at 12pm, while Tunisia and Japan will go head-to-head at 4pm.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Ecuador and Curaçao locked at 0-0 at half-time
item.description: Ecuador and Curaçao remain locked at 0-0 at half-time in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group E clash, with both sides still searching for the breakthrough. Ecuador has enjoyed more possession and created several attacking opportunities in the opening 45 minutes, but Curaçao's defence has stood firm to keep the match scoreless heading into the break.
item.content:encoded:
Ecuador and Curaçao remain locked at 0-0 at half-time in their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group E clash, with both sides still searching for the breakthrough.

Ecuador has enjoyed more possession and created several attacking opportunities in the opening 45 minutes, but Curaçao's defence has stood firm to keep the match scoreless heading into the break.

For Ecuador, this marks their fifth FIFA World Cup appearance after previously qualifying in 2002, 2006, 2014 and 2022. Their best performance came in 2006 when they reached the Round of 16.

Curaçao, meanwhile, is making history with its first-ever FIFA World Cup appearance. The Caribbean nation secured qualification for the tournament for the first time and is looking to make a strong impression on football's biggest stage.

Both teams entered today's match seeking their first points of the tournament after opening defeats in Group E, making the second half crucial for their hopes of advancing to the knockout stages.

Meanwhile, Netherlands hammered Sweden 5-1 and Sweden defeated Ivory Coast 2-1 earlier this morning.

Tunisia and Japan will go head-to-head at 4pm.

Stay with us for updates on the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Boks deliver eighty-point statement ahead of test season
item.description: Swire Shipping Fijian Drua centre Virimi Vakatawa helped set up a try for his Barbarians last night, but it was not enough as they lost to South Africa 80-31. The Springboks began their 2026 international season with an impressive victory over the Barbarians in Port Elizabeth.
item.content:encoded:
Swire Shipping Fijian Drua centre Virimi Vakatawa helped set up a try for his Barbarians last night, but it was not enough as they lost to South Africa 80-31.

The Springboks began their 2026 international season with an impressive victory over the Barbarians in Port Elizabeth.

The match featured exciting attacking rugby, with both teams scoring freely in a fast-paced first half that produced 10 tries.

South Africa led 40-26 at halftime and added another 40 points in the second half, finishing with 12 tries overall.

Wing Edwill van der Merwe starred with a hat-trick, while Cheslin Kolbe contributed 25 points with his kicking and a try of his own.

Although the scoreline was one-sided, the Barbarians were far more competitive than in their previous meeting with the Boks, showing resilience and attacking flair throughout.

Strong performances from Jasper Wiese, Siya Kolisi, Pieter-Steph du Toit, and debutant Riley Norton were key highlights for South Africa.nt Riley Norton were key highlights for South Africa.

The match provided valuable preparation for the Springboks ahead of their upcoming Test season, which begins against England in Johannesburg in two weeks.

item.title: Paraguay beat Turkey 1-nil to keep World Cup hopes alive
item.description: Ten-man Paraguay kept their FIFA World Cup knockout-stage hopes alive after edging Turkey 1-0 in a dramatic Group D encounter, whi
